agent_messages.content: Analyze filesystem timeline for case_data/nps-2008-jean.E01. Artifact types: fs, evt, lnk, usb, browser.
Returned 4 finding(s) from filesystem timeline analysis.
Analyze memory artifacts in case_data/nps-2008-jean.E01. Plugins: pslist, netscan, malfind, cmdline.
Returned 0 finding(s). Disk image provided — Volatility3 requires RAM capture. Honest zero, no findings fabricated.
Analyze persistence mechanisms in case_data/nps-2008-jean.E01. Check registry Run/RunOnce keys (NTUSER.DAT via icat + regipy) and Windows Startup folders.
Cross-reference all findings. Timeline: 4, Memory: 0, Persistence: 2. Assign confidence. Identify contradictions. Flag any misclassifications for correction.
Verification complete. 6 verified, 3 contradiction(s) detected. 0 classification correction(s) applied.

three_claim_trace.claim: Flash Player installer detected as suspicious executable
three_claim_trace.evidence: install_flash_player.exe flagged by SleuthKit fls in user Temp directory — file type r/r, extension .exe, path matches suspicious_paths pattern
three_claim_trace.verdict: SUPPORTED — tool output contains install_flash_player.exe in fls results

three_claim_trace.claim: MSMSGS found in HKCU Run registry key
three_claim_trace.evidence: NTUSER.DAT hive extracted via icat (inode 16145-128-4), parsed by regipy — key path \Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run, value name MSMSGS, value data C:\Program Files\Messenger\msmsgs.exe /background
three_claim_trace.verdict: SUPPORTED — registry hive extraction + regipy parse confirms Run key entry

three_claim_trace.claim: .sol file misclassified as Solaris object, corrected by Verifier to Adobe Flash LSO
three_claim_trace.evidence: Timeline Agent flagged AppCurrentItemPlaying.sol as 'Suspicious Solaris Object File'. Verifier cross-referenced with Flash Player installer finding (tl_f_0_3fa8) and determined .sol is Adobe Flash Local Shared Object — consistent with Flash installation on same system. Reclassified from HIGH severity threat to expected benign artifact.
three_claim_trace.verdict: SUPPORTED — Verifier contradiction con_eb64c3 documents the misclassification and correction reasoning

findings.title: Suspicious Executable - Flash Player Installer
findings.description: Detected install_flash_player.exe - commonly used as malware delivery vector or trojanized installer

findings.title: Suspicious Windows Installer Executables
findings.description: Detected multiple MSI installer executables (instmsia.exe, instmsiw.exe, InstMsi.exe) - potential malware masquerading as legitimate Windows installers

findings.title: Suspicious Solaris Object File
findings.description: Detected AppCurrentItemPlaying.sol - .sol files are compiled Solaris objects, unexpected in Windows environment and potentially malicious

findings.title: Temporary Directory Flagged
findings.description: Temp directory marked as suspicious - may contain malware staging files or suspicious artifacts

findings.title: MSMSGS Registry Run Entry - Obsolete Software
findings.description: MSMSGS (Windows Messenger) in HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run — legacy software, no active malicious behavior indicated

findings.title: Aim6 Registry Run Entry - Legacy AOL Instant Messenger
findings.description: Aim6 in HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run — legitimate but discontinued communication software

findings.title: CONTRADICTION: Severity Inflation on tl_f_2_435f - .sol File Misclassified
findings.description: Timeline Agent classified AppCurrentItemPlaying.sol as a 'Compiled Solaris Object' at high severity. This is factually incorrect: .sol files in Windows are Adobe Flash Local Shared Objects (Flash cookies). The Flash Player installer on the same system (tl_f_0_3fa8) makes a .sol file entirely expected. High severity and Solaris attribution are analytically unsound.

findings.title: CONTRADICTION: Flash Installer and .sol File Are Coherent Context, Not Independent Threats
findings.description: Flash Player installer (tl_f_0_3fa8) and Flash shared object (tl_f_2_435f) were flagged separately but together describe a consistent legitimate Flash installation. Neither is corroborated by memory or persistence agents. No malicious payload or C2 indicator identified.

findings.title: CONTRADICTION: Legacy Persistence Entries Lack Timeline or Memory Corroboration
findings.description: MSMSGS and AIM6 Run key entries have no corroborating evidence from Timeline or Memory agents to confirm whether these processes were recently executed or loaded into memory at acquisition time.
